replaced: REPLACED: Fragmented personal tools (Notion, Google Calendar, Gmail, LinkedIn, scattered AI prompts)
name: Personal Multi-Agent Operating Company
description: Didn't just use AI tools. Built the company that runs my life. Three executive AI agents — Buttercup (Chief of Staff), Blossom (CFO), Bubbles (CBO) — each routing to 2-5 specialized staff agents. Buttercup is a live WhatsApp interface via Twilio webhook. Staff handles inbox triage, email drafting, calendar management, job scouting, AI signals, LinkedIn drafting, website updates, and financial tracking. Architecture: hub-and-spoke, max depth 2, least-privilege tool grants, fresh context per run. Built on Claude agents with MCP integrations across Gmail, Google Calendar, Indeed, and Vercel.

name: Internal AI CRM — Deal Slack Ops
description: The legacy CRM is a $200B industry. We're building the replacement internally. This is Sigma's AI-native CRM suite — Deal Slack Ops is the first shipped app: Slack channels auto-created per opportunity, linked to account and deal context at creation.

replaced: REPLACED: Pipeline intelligence + conversation analytics tools
name: GTM AI Stack $100K SaaS Replacement
description: Replaced external GTM SaaS platforms with warehouse-native AI systems built on Snowflake Cortex and LLM APIs. Outcome: $100K+ annual cost savings. Pipeline intelligence, forecasting narratives, lead enrichment, buyer-intent scoring, exec reporting automation.

replaced: REPLACED: Dependence on a single engineer
name: Internal AI Frameworks + Playbooks
description: Built reusable AI app frameworks and API-driven automation pipelines. Then documented them so non-engineering teams could build AI workflows without buying new SaaS. The goal: make the internal AI capability compounding, not dependent on a single engineer.
